name: Quantum Fingerprinting
description: Exponentially compress a classical string into a quantum fingerprint that enables equality testing.
long_description: Quantum fingerprinting encodes an n-bit classical string into a O(log n)-qubit quantum state such that two parties can test string equality using only a constant number of qubits of communication. The referee runs a SWAP test on the two fingerprints. This circuit shows fingerprints for strings '0' and '0' (identical): the SWAP test ancilla should measure 0 with certainty. For different strings, it measures 1 with probability (1 - |⟨f₁|f₂⟩|²)/2 > 0.

references.title: Buhrman et al. (2001). Quantum Fingerprinting. PRL 87, 167902

author: OpenQC Community
